name: Nirvanamed.pt: WordPress site operations
description: WordPress-based clinic site tree with PHP 8.3 compatibility work, local dev notes, and asset packaging—focused on maintainable hosting operations.
longDescription: WordPress deployment layout with core structure, themes and plugins under wp-content, and supporting tooling, plus technical notes for PHP 8.3 alignment, layout debugging, and local development. The focus is long-running site maintenance rather than a greenfield SPA: traceable fixes, verification notes, and repeatable workflows for the team running the site.
features: Versioned site tree suitable for staged updates and audits
Technical write-ups for compatibility and layout verification
Separation between packaged assets and working directories
Operational documentation alongside the PHP application
role: Maintenance, PHP upgrades, and deployment hygiene
highlights: Emphasis on traceable change history for a live WordPress property
PHP version migration support material captured in-repo
Practical packaging patterns for backups and hand-offs
challenges: Long-lived WordPress installs accumulate theme/plugin drift and hosting-specific constraints; upgrades must be verifiable without exposing internal credentials in docs.
solutions: Keep operational notes and verification artifacts in version control while storing secrets only in server environment configuration; prefer documented checklists over ad-hoc production edits.
security: Credentials and connection strings belong only in host configuration, not in public documentation
Treat wp-content and custom modules as part of the attack surface—updates and reviews are first-class
